cm.sv: Let brotherly love continue. Que el amor fraternal continúe.

Don´t forget to show hospitality to strangers, for in doing so, some have entertained angels without knowing it. No olviden la hospitalidad con los extranjeros, pues con esto, algunos han hospedado ángeles sin saberlo.

Remember those who are in bonds, as bound with them; and those who are ill-treated, since you are also in the body. Recuerden a los prisioneros, como encadenados con ellos; y a los maltratados, como si ustedes también estuvieran en el cuerpo.

Let marriage be held in honor among all, and let the bed be undefiled: but God will judge the sexually immoral and adulterers. Que el matrimonio sea honorable en todo, y la cama limpia pero Dios juzgará a los inmorales sexuales y a los adulteros.

Be free from the love of money, content with such things as you have, for he has said, «I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.» Sea la costumbre sencilla contentos con lo presente, pues Él ha dicho, «Nunca te dejaré, ni de forma alguna te abandonaré.»

So that with good courage we say, Para que nosotros podamos decir con valor,
«The Lord is my helper. I will not fear. «El Señor es mi auxilio, y no temeré.
What can man do to me?» ¿Qué puede hacerme el hombre?

Remember your leaders, men who spoke to you the word of God, and considering the results of their conduct, imitate their faith. Recuerden a sus líderes, quienes les hablaron la palabra de Dios, cuya fe deben imitar, considerando el resultado de su conducta.
Jesus Christ is the same yesterday, today, and forever. Jesús Cristo es el mismo ayer, y hoy y por siempre.

Don´t be carried away by various and strange teachings, for it is good that the heart be established by grace, not by food, through which those who were so occupied were not benefited. No se dejen llevar por diversas y extrañas enseñanzas, pues es bueno que el corazón se establezca en la gracia, no por comida, que no fue de provecho para quienes se ocuparon de esta.
We have an altar from which those who serve the holy tabernacle have no right to eat. Tenemos un altar del cual no tienen derecho a comer quienes sirven al tabernáculo.

For the bodies of those animals, whose blood is brought into the holy place by the high priest as an offering for sin, are burned outside of the camp. Porque los cuerpos de aquellos animales, cuya sangre es traída al lugar santo por el sumo sacerdote por el pecado, son quemados fuera del campamento.

Therefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people through his own blood, suffered outside of the gate. Entonces Jesús también, para santificar a la gente mediante su sangre, sufrió fuera de la puerta.

Let us therefore go forth to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach. Vayamos entonces a Él fuera del campamento, cargando su oprobio dice reproche, dice `vituperio,´ según la palabra griega `ὀνειδισμὸν´ puede traducirse como reproche, como el que Cristo sufrió por Dios a manos de sus enemigos. .
For we don´t have here an enduring city, but we seek that which is to come. Pues no tenemos aquí una ciudad permanente más buscamos la que vendrá.

Through him, then, let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips which make confession to his name. Mediante Él, entonces, ofrezcamos continuamente un sacrificio de alabanza a Dios, esto es, el fruto de los labios que confiesan Su nombre.
But don´t forget to be doing good and sharing, for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

Obey your leaders and submit to them, for they watch on behalf of your souls, as those who will give account, that they may do this with joy, and not with groaning, for that would be unprofitable for you.
Pray for us, for we are persuaded that we have a good conscience, desiring to live honorably in all things.
I strongly urge you to do this, that I may be restored to you sooner.
Now may the God of peace, who brought again from the dead the great shepherd of the sheep with the blood of an eternal covenant, our Lord Jesus,
make you complete in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is well pleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ, to whom be the glory forever and ever. Amen.
But I exhort you, brothers, endure the word of exhortation, for I have written to you in few words.
Know that our brother Timothy has been freed, with whom, if he comes shortly, I will see you.
Greet all of your leaders and all the saints. The Italians greet you.
Grace be with you all. Amen.
